user_prompt:
Google Cloud OAuth setup for gmailctl — step-by-step

  This is a one-time setup per Google account. Allow ~10 minutes. You'll end up with ~/.gmailctl/credentials.json + ~/.gmailctl/token.json, both gitignored.

  Step 1 — Create the local directory

  mkdir -p ~/.gmailctl

  You'll save two files here over the course of the setup.

  Step 2 — Install gmailctl (if you haven't)

  brew install gmailctl
  gmailctl --version    # should print v0.10.x or later

  Step 3 — Open Google Cloud Console

  Go to https://console.cloud.google.com/

  Sign in with the same Google account whose Gmail you want to filter (i.e. the inbox you want gmailctl to manage). If you have multiple Google accounts, double-check the
  top-right account switcher.

  Step 4 — Create a new Cloud project

  1. Click the project dropdown at the top-left of the page (next to "Google Cloud" logo).
  2. In the modal, click NEW PROJECT (top-right of the modal).
  3. Project name: mail-filters-personal (anything works; this never appears to end users).
  4. Organization: leave as No organization (unless you have a Workspace org you specifically want this under).
  5. Click CREATE.

  Wait ~10 seconds for the project to spin up. The console will switch to the new project automatically — confirm via the dropdown.

  Step 5 — Enable the Gmail API

  1. In the left sidebar (☰ menu), go to APIs & Services → Library.                                                                                                         
   ─-─Direct─URL:─https://console.cloud.google.com/apis/library────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  2. In the search bar, type Gmail API.
  3. Click the Gmail API result (Google's tile).
  4. Click the blue ENABLE button.                                                                                        Update available! Run: brew upgrade claude-code

  Wait ~5 seconds for the enable to register. You'll be redirected to the Gmail API overview page.

  Step 6 — Configure the OAuth consent screen

  This is what users see when granting access. Since you're the only user, it'll be minimal.

  1. In the left sidebar: APIs & Services → OAuth consent screen.
    - Direct URL: https://console.cloud.google.com/apis/credentials/consent
  2. User Type: select External → click CREATE.
    - (Internal is only available for Workspace accounts; External is correct for personal Gmail.)
  3. OAuth consent screen (page 1 of the form):
    - App name: mail-filters (anything; this is what you'll see when granting access)
    - User support email: pick your email from the dropdown.
    - Developer contact information → Email addresses: your email again.
    - Leave everything else blank.
    - Click SAVE AND CONTINUE.
  4. Scopes (page 2):
    - Click ADD OR REMOVE SCOPES.
    - In the side panel that opens, in the filter box type: gmail.
    - Check these two scopes:
        - https://www.googleapis.com/auth/gmail.la
[...truncated for length...]
